title: Disjoint Sets, Connectivity, and Offline Grouping · Scale Transfer
summary: Track changing network connectivity across merges without scanning every member. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a union-find service with measured compression with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent disjoint sets as parent forests with explicit representative invariants.
Apply union by rank or size without corrupting representative metadata.
Trace path compression and explain its interaction with later finds.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
